div1.entryFree:
Wheel, vb. 1) to turn round on an axis: thus hath the course of justice --ed about, R3 IV, 4, 105 (Ff whirl'd).
2) to fetch a compass; and hence to err about: I was forced to w. three or four miles about, Cor. I, 6, 19. attend me where I w. Troil. V, 7, 2. an extravagant and --ing stranger of here and everywhere, Oth. I, 1, 137.
